description_long: This is a vintage can opener-style wrench with a robust, dark wooden handle and a metal head. The handle is cylindrical, showing signs of wear and a slightly weathered texture, indicating frequent use. The metal head features a hooked, pointed blade and a flat, prying edge, both darkened with age and use. The transition between the handle and the metal head is secured with a visible rivet. The metal surface has a matte, slightly rough finish, with some minor rust and discoloration. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for opening cans or prying objects in an urban or garage setting.
description: A vintage can opener-style wrench with a worn wooden handle and a dark, aged metal head featuring a hooked blade and prying edge.
description_view_0: The wooden handle is prominent, with the hooked metal blade and prying edge partially visible at the front.
description_view_1: The side profile highlights the full length of the handle and the distinct hooked and flat edges of the metal head.
description_view_2: The top-down view shows the alignment of the handle with the metal head, emphasizing the hooked blade and prying edge.
description_view_3: The underside reveals the curvature of the metal head and the weathered texture of the wooden handle.
attribute_rust_or_wear_marks: Minor rust and wear marks are visible on the metal head, especially near the blade and prying edge. The wooden handle shows signs of wear and discoloration.
